598	// normalize parses the string-typed fields (addresses) into their typed forms.
599	func (c *Config) normalize() error {
600		for i := range c.Paths {
601			p := &c.Paths[i]
602			if p.SourceAddrRaw != "" {
603				addr, err := netip.ParseAddr(p.SourceAddrRaw)
604				if err != nil {
605					return fmt.Errorf("path %q: invalid source_addr %q: %w", p.Name, p.SourceAddrRaw, err)
606				}
607				p.SourceAddr = addr
608			}
609			if p.DestAddrRaw != "" {
610				dst, err := netip.ParseAddrPort(p.DestAddrRaw)
611				if err != nil {
612					return fmt.Errorf("path %q: invalid dest_addr %q: %w", p.Name, p.DestAddrRaw, err)
613				}
614				p.DestAddr = dst
615			}
616			if p.LinkBandwidthRaw != "" {
617				bw, err := parseBandwidth(p.LinkBandwidthRaw)
618				if err != nil {
619					return fmt.Errorf("path %q: invalid link_bandwidth %q: %w", p.Name, p.LinkBandwidthRaw, err)
620				}
621				if bw <= 0 {
622					return fmt.Errorf("path %q: link_bandwidth must be > 0, got %q", p.Name, p.LinkBandwidthRaw)
623				}
624				p.LinkBandwidthBitsPerSec = bw
625			}
626			if p.LinkRTTRaw != "" {
627				rtt, err := time.ParseDuration(p.LinkRTTRaw)
628				if err != nil {
629					return fmt.Errorf("path %q: invalid link_rtt %q: %w", p.Name, p.LinkRTTRaw, err)
630				}
631				if rtt <= 0 {
632					return fmt.Errorf("path %q: link_rtt must be > 0, got %q", p.Name, p.LinkRTTRaw)
633				}
634				p.LinkRTT = rtt
635			}
636		}
637		for i := range c.WireGuard.Peers {
638			if err := c.WireGuard.Peers[i].resolveEndpoints(); err != nil {
639				return fmt.Errorf("wireguard peer %d: %w", i, err)
640			}
641		}
642		c.Amnezia.applyDefaults()
643		// Derive the weighted pace from any operator-declared per-link bandwidth BEFORE
644		// applyDefaults, so it can (a) see the raw zero PerPathCapacityFPS to distinguish an
645		// explicit knob from the default and (b) set the derived capacity/burst that
646		// applyDefaults then leaves intact (it only fills a knob left at zero). Under a
647		// disabled or non-weighted pacing it is a no-op, so the synthetic default is kept.
648		if err := c.deriveWeightedPacingFromBDP(); err != nil {
649			return err
650		}
651		c.Scheduler.applyDefaults()
652		c.FEC.applyDefaults()
653		return nil
654	}
655	
656	// deriveWeightedPacingFromBDP sizes the weighted scheduler's per-path pace from the
657	// operator-declared per-link bandwidth (T53, Q20) via SizePacingFromBDP instead of the
658	// synthetic defaultPerPathCapacityFPS. It runs only under the weighted policy with
659	// pacing ENABLED and at least one declared link_bandwidth; otherwise a declared
660	// bandwidth is inert (pacing ships DISABLED by default), so an unrelated config is
661	// untouched. The value is OPERATOR-DECLARED and fixed at load — NOT runtime auto-tuning
662	// (Q20 rejected a live control loop for the pilot).
663	//
664	// The scheduler carries a SINGLE reference per-path capacity applied to every path's
665	// token bucket, so a heterogeneous link set is sized to the BOTTLENECK: the slowest
666	// declared link governs the shared pace, because pacing any path faster than the
667	// slowest link's capacity would let that link build the very standing queue pacing
668	// exists to prevent. All paths must therefore declare a bandwidth (all-or-nothing) so
669	// the bottleneck is well-defined across the whole path set.
670	func (c *Config) deriveWeightedPacingFromBDP() error {
671		s := &c.Scheduler
672		if s.Policy != PolicyWeighted || !s.PacingEnabled {
673			return nil
674		}
675		declared := 0
676		for i := range c.Paths {
677			if c.Paths[i].LinkBandwidthBitsPerSec > 0 {
678				declared++
679			}
680		}
681		if declared == 0 {
682			return nil // no declared bandwidth: the synthetic default pace is preserved.
683		}
684		if declared != len(c.Paths) {
685			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler pacing: link_bandwidth must be declared on ALL paths or none (got %d of %d) — the shared per-path pace is sized to the slowest declared link, which is undefined with a partial declaration", declared, len(c.Paths))
686		}
687		if s.PerPathCapacityFPS != 0 || s.PacingBurstFrames != 0 {
688			return errors.New("scheduler.per_path_capacity_fps / pacing_burst_frames and per-path link_bandwidth are mutually exclusive: declare the link bandwidth (BDP-derived pace) OR set the raw frame-slot knobs, not both")
689		}
690		var bottleneck BDPSizing
691		for i := range c.Paths {
692			p := &c.Paths[i]
693			if p.LinkRTT <= 0 {
694				return fmt.Errorf("path %q: link_rtt is required (> 0) when link_bandwidth is set under weighted pacing — it is the delay term of the bandwidth-delay-product burst", p.Name)
695			}
696			sz, err := SizePacingFromBDP(p.LinkBandwidthBitsPerSec, p.LinkRTT, defaultAvgWireFrameBytes)
697			if err != nil {
698				return fmt.Errorf("path %q: %w", p.Name, err)
699			}
700			if i == 0 || sz.CapacityFPS < bottleneck.CapacityFPS {
701				bottleneck = sz
702			}
703		}
704		s.PerPathCapacityFPS = bottleneck.CapacityFPS
705		s.PacingBurstFrames = bottleneck.BurstFrames
706		return nil
707	}
708	
709	// applyDefaults selects active-backup when no policy is given and, only under the
710	// weighted policy, fills any weighted knob left at its zero value with its default.
711	// It is a no-op for the active-backup policy: those weighted knobs are inert there,
712	// so leaving them zero keeps the config surface for a P1 deployment empty.
713	func (s *SchedulerConfig) applyDefaults() {
714		if s.Policy == "" {
715			s.Policy = PolicyActiveBackup
716		}
717		if s.Policy != PolicyWeighted {
718			return
719		}
720		if s.PerPathCapacityFPS == 0 {
721			s.PerPathCapacityFPS = defaultPerPathCapacityFPS
722		}
723		if s.EngageFraction == 0 {
724			s.EngageFraction = defaultEngageFraction
725		}
726		if s.DisengageFraction == 0 {
727			s.DisengageFraction = defaultDisengageFraction
728		}
729		if s.CollapseDwell == 0 {
730			s.CollapseDwell = defaultCollapseDwell
731		}
732		if s.LoadTau == 0 {
733			s.LoadTau = defaultLoadTau
734		}
735		if s.PacingBurstFrames == 0 {
736			s.PacingBurstFrames = defaultPacingBurstFrames
737		}
738		if s.WeightRTTFloor == 0 {
739			s.WeightRTTFloor = defaultWeightRTTFloor
740		}
741		if s.WeightLossFloor == 0 {
742			s.WeightLossFloor = defaultWeightLossFloor
743		}
744	}
745	
746	// validate enforces the scheduler policy invariants. active-backup needs no tuning.
747	// The weighted policy fails fast on any out-of-range knob so a mis-tuned
748	// aggregation policy is rejected at load rather than misbehaving at runtime (the
749	// hysteresis band, in particular, is only a band when disengage < engage).
750	func (s SchedulerConfig) validate() error {
751		if !s.Policy.valid() {
752			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler.policy must be %q or %q, got %q", PolicyActiveBackup, PolicyWeighted, s.Policy)
753		}
754		if s.Policy != PolicyWeighted {
755			return nil
756		}
757		if s.PerPathCapacityFPS <= 0 {
758			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler.per_path_capacity_fps must be > 0 under the weighted policy, got %g", s.PerPathCapacityFPS)
759		}
760		if s.EngageFraction <= 0 || s.EngageFraction > 1 {
761			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler.engage_fraction must be in (0,1], got %g", s.EngageFraction)
762		}
763		if s.DisengageFraction < 0 || s.DisengageFraction >= s.EngageFraction {
764			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler.disengage_fraction must be in [0,engage_fraction=%g) to form a hysteresis band, got %g", s.EngageFraction, s.DisengageFraction)
765		}
766		if s.CollapseDwell < 0 {
767			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler.collapse_dwell must be >= 0, got %s", s.CollapseDwell)
768		}
769		if s.LoadTau <= 0 {
770			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler.load_tau must be > 0, got %s", s.LoadTau)
771		}
772		if s.PacingEnabled && s.PacingBurstFrames <= 0 {
773			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler.pacing_burst_frames must be > 0 when pacing is enabled, got %g", s.PacingBurstFrames)
774		}
775		if s.WeightRTTFloor <= 0 {
776			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler.weight_rtt_floor must be > 0 under the weighted policy, got %s", s.WeightRTTFloor)
777		}
778		if s.WeightLossFloor <= 0 {
779			return fmt.Errorf("scheduler.weight_loss_floor must be > 0 under the weighted policy, got %g", s.WeightLossFloor)
780		}
781		return nil
782	}
783	
784	// validate enforces the required-field invariants, failing on the first problem.
785	func (c *Config) validate() error {
786		if !c.Role.valid() {
787			return fmt.Errorf("role must be %q or %q, got %q", RoleEdge, RoleConcentrator, c.Role)
788		}
789		if len(c.Paths) == 0 {
790			return errors.New("at least one path is required")
791		}
792		seen := make(map[string]struct{}, len(c.Paths))
793		// seenSrc maps an already-claimed source_addr to the path that claimed it, so a
794		// second path reusing it can be rejected at LOAD naming both conflicting paths.
795		// The multipath bind Opens each path on (source_addr, listen_port), so two paths
796		// sharing a source_addr collide EADDRINUSE at the second ListenUDP (and on every
797		// re-Open after Down/Up, since the engine passes the fixed port back) — a
798		// misconfiguration that must fail fast at load, not at bring-up (defect D10).
799		seenSrc := make(map[netip.Addr]string, len(c.Paths))
800		for i, p := range c.Paths {
801			if p.Name == "" {
802				return fmt.Errorf("path %d: name is required", i)
803			}
804			if _, dup := seen[p.Name]; dup {
805				return fmt.Errorf("duplicate path name %q", p.Name)
806			}
807			seen[p.Name] = struct{}{}
808			if !p.SourceAddr.IsValid() {
809				return fmt.Errorf("path %q: source_addr is required", p.Name)
810			}
811			// Compare the UNMAPPED form: "192.0.2.10" (Is4) and "::ffff:192.0.2.10"
812			// (Is4In6) are distinct under netip == yet bind the identical v4 socket, so
813			// the two spellings must collide in the guard exactly as they do at bind.
814			src := p.SourceAddr.Unmap()
815			if prev, dup := seenSrc[src]; dup {
816				return fmt.Errorf("paths %q and %q share source_addr %s; each path must bind a distinct source address (a shared source collides EADDRINUSE at the second bind)", prev, p.Name, p.SourceAddr)
817			}
818			seenSrc[src] = p.Name
819		}
820		if !c.WireGuard.PrivateKey.IsSet() {
821			return errors.New("wireguard.private_key is required")
822		}
823		if len(c.WireGuard.Peers) == 0 {
824			return errors.New("at least one wireguard peer is required")
825		}
826		for i, peer := range c.WireGuard.Peers {
827			if !peer.PublicKey.IsSet() {
828				return fmt.Errorf("wireguard peer %d: public_key is required", i)
829			}
830			if c.Role == RoleEdge && len(peer.Endpoints) == 0 {
831				return fmt.Errorf("wireguard peer %d: endpoint is required for the edge role", i)
832			}
833			// The concentrator learns the edge's endpoint dynamically (roaming across
834			// NAT rebinds); an ordered failover endpoint list is meaningless for it, so
835			// reject rather than silently ignore a mis-targeted config (Q18 is edge-side
836			// only).
837			if c.Role == RoleConcentrator && len(peer.Endpoints) != 0 {
838				return fmt.Errorf("wireguard peer %d: endpoint/endpoints is not meaningful for the concentrator role (it learns the edge's endpoint dynamically)", i)
839			}
840		}
841		if c.Role == RoleConcentrator && c.WireGuard.ListenPort == 0 {
842			return errors.New("wireguard.listen_port is required for the concentrator role")
843		}
844		if !c.PSK.IsSet() {
845			return errors.New("psk is required (authenticates outer control/probe frames)")
846		}
847		if err := c.Amnezia.validate(); err != nil {
848			return err
849		}
850		if err := c.Scheduler.validate(); err != nil {
851			return err
852		}
853		if err := c.FEC.validate(); err != nil {
854			return err
855		}
856		return nil
857	}
858	
859	// validate enforces the amnezia obfuscation invariants (defect D1). An
860	// unconfigured block is valid (plain WireGuard). A configured block must specify
861	// the WHOLE junk/size set and carry a consistent magic-header set, so a partial
862	// or inconsistent obfuscation profile FAILS FAST at load rather than producing a
863	// silently mismatched tunnel that never handshakes.
864	//
865	// This runs after applyDefaults, so an omitted magic-header set has already been
866	// filled with the standard 1..4 values; the header check below therefore only
867	// fires for a genuinely partial header set (some given, some left zero).
868	func (a Amnezia) validate() error {
869		if !a.Configured() {
870			return nil
871		}
872		// All-or-nothing: enabling amnezia at all requires the full junk/size set, so
873		// both ends are forced to specify the same complete profile. A partial block
874		// (e.g. jc/jmin/jmax set but s1/s2 omitted) would leave the ends deriving
875		// different profiles and the handshake would fail closed with no diagnostic.
876		if a.Jc <= 0 || a.Jmin <= 0 || a.Jmax <= 0 || a.S1 <= 0 || a.S2 <= 0 {
877			return fmt.Errorf("amnezia: incomplete obfuscation set — when configured, jc, jmin, jmax, s1 and s2 must all be > 0 (got jc=%d jmin=%d jmax=%d s1=%d s2=%d)",
878				a.Jc, a.Jmin, a.Jmax, a.S1, a.S2)
879		}
880		if a.Jmin > a.Jmax {
881			return fmt.Errorf("amnezia: require jmin <= jmax, got jmin=%d jmax=%d", a.Jmin, a.Jmax)
882		}
883		// Junk sizes must not collide: amneziawg-go classifies an incoming datagram's
884		// message type by its on-the-wire length, so the obfuscated initiation packet
885		// (MessageInitiationSize + s1) and response packet (MessageResponseSize + s2)
886		// must differ in length. A colliding s1/s2 pair passes config load but is
887		// rejected later by the engine's IpcSet ("new init size == new response size");
888		// reject it here so the collision fails fast at load, at the right locus. The
889		// engine's own MessageInitiationSize (148) and MessageResponseSize (92)
890		// constants are used so this check tracks the vendored fork if it ever changes.
891		if awgdevice.MessageInitiationSize+a.S1 == awgdevice.MessageResponseSize+a.S2 {
892			return fmt.Errorf("amnezia: junk sizes collide — obfuscated init size %d (%d+s1) must differ from response size %d (%d+s2); adjust s1/s2",
893				awgdevice.MessageInitiationSize+a.S1, awgdevice.MessageInitiationSize,
894				awgdevice.MessageResponseSize+a.S2, awgdevice.MessageResponseSize)
895		}
896		// Magic headers must be distinct: the receive path classifies a datagram's
897		// message type by its header value, so two equal headers make two message
898		// types indistinguishable. An all-zero header set is left for applyDefaults;
899		// any non-zero header requires a complete, distinct set (a partial set leaves
900		// zeros here and is caught as a duplicate).
901		if a.H1 != 0 || a.H2 != 0 || a.H3 != 0 || a.H4 != 0 {
902			if a.H1 == a.H2 || a.H1 == a.H3 || a.H1 == a.H4 ||
903				a.H2 == a.H3 || a.H2 == a.H4 || a.H3 == a.H4 {
904				return fmt.Errorf("amnezia: magic headers must be a complete, distinct set, got h1=%d h2=%d h3=%d h4=%d",
905					a.H1, a.H2, a.H3, a.H4)
906			}
907		}
908		return nil
909	}

3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"os/exec"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"strconv"
11		"strings"
12		"syscall"
13		"testing"
14		"time"
15	)
16	
17	// TestRuntimePathReload is the T30 e2e acceptance: the bond comes up with ONE path,
18	// a second path is added at runtime via a SIGHUP config reload and brought into the
19	// scheduler once healthy — with the in-flight TCP flow surviving with ZERO reset —
20	// then the second path is removed via another SIGHUP while the flow continues on the
21	// remaining path, the WG session and the surviving path undisturbed throughout.
22	//
23	// It is gated behind the e2e build tag (netns + root) and is exercised for
24	// COMPILATION in CI via `go test -tags e2e -c`.
25	func TestRuntimePathReload(t *testing.T) {
26		bin := buildWanbond(t)
27		top := Setup(t)
28	
29		starlink := top.path("starlink")
30		cellular := top.path("cellular")
31	
32		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
33		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
34		psk := randKey(t)
35	
36		dir := t.TempDir()
37		edgeCfgPath := filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml")
38		concCfgPath := filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml")
39	
40		// Start with only the Starlink path configured on both ends.
41		writeConfig(t, edgeCfgPath, edgeConfigBody(psk, edgePriv, concPub, []pathSpec{starlink}))
42		writeConfig(t, concCfgPath, concConfigBody(psk, concPriv, edgePub, []pathSpec{starlink}))
43	
44		conc := top.startProc(t, "concentrator", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfgPath)
45		edge := top.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfgPath)
46	
47		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
48			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
49		}
50		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
51			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
52		}
53		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
54		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
55		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
56		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
57	
58		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
59			t.Fatalf("single-path bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
60		}
61	
62		// Start a long-lived TCP flow across the tunnel; it must survive the add/remove
63		// with ZERO reset (a TCP reset aborts the iperf3 client with a non-zero exit). The
64		// one-shot server and the t.Cleanup it registers run on the MAIN test goroutine —
65		// startProc/runOut may t.Fatalf, which is illegal off the test goroutine — and the
66		// client's result is sent back over a channel to be asserted on the main goroutine,
67		// mirroring baseline_test.go's rttUnderLoad.
68		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", concInner)
69		type flowResult struct {
70			out []byte
71			err error
72		}
73		flowCh := make(chan flowResult, 1)
74		go func() {
75			time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
76			out, err := exec.Command("iperf3", "-c", concInner, "-t", "8", "-J").CombinedOutput()
77			flowCh <- flowResult{out: out, err: err}
78		}()
79	
80		// --- Add the cellular path at runtime via SIGHUP on both ends. ---
81		writeReload(t, edgeCfgPath, edgeConfigBody(psk, edgePriv, concPub, []pathSpec{starlink, cellular}))
82		writeReload(t, concCfgPath, concConfigBody(psk, concPriv, edgePub, []pathSpec{starlink, cellular}))
83		sighup(t, conc)
84		sighup(t, edge)
85	
86		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 10*time.Second) {
87			t.Fatalf("bond lost connectivity after adding the cellular path at runtime\n--- edge ---\n%s", edge.log())
88		}
89	
90		// --- Remove the cellular path at runtime; the flow continues on Starlink. ---
91		writeReload(t, edgeCfgPath, edgeConfigBody(psk, edgePriv, concPub, []pathSpec{starlink}))
92		writeReload(t, concCfgPath, concConfigBody(psk, concPriv, edgePub, []pathSpec{starlink}))
93		sighup(t, conc)
94		sighup(t, edge)
95	
96		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 10*time.Second) {
97			t.Fatalf("bond lost connectivity after removing the cellular path at runtime\n--- edge ---\n%s", edge.log())
98		}
99	
100		// ZERO-RESET acceptance, asserted EXPLICITLY on the main goroutine: the long-lived
101		// TCP flow that spanned the runtime add AND remove ran to completion. A TCP reset
102		// (the failure this test guards against) aborts iperf3 with a non-zero exit, so
103		// err==nil proves the connection was never reset, and a positive transferred rate
104		// proves data actually flowed across the whole reconfiguration window.
105		res := <-flowCh
106		if res.err != nil {
107			t.Fatalf("in-flight TCP flow did not survive the runtime path add/remove (a TCP reset aborts iperf3): %v\n%s\n--- edge ---\n%s",
108				res.err, res.out, edge.log())
109		}
110		mbps := parseIperfSentMbps(t, res.out)
111		if mbps <= 0 {
112			t.Fatalf("in-flight TCP flow transferred no data across the runtime add/remove:\n%s", res.out)
113		}
114		t.Logf("runtime path reload: single-path up, +cellular via SIGHUP, -cellular via SIGHUP; in-flight TCP flow survived with zero reset (%.1f Mbit/s)", mbps)
115	}
116	
117	// edgeConfigBody renders the edge TOML for the given path set: one source-bound
118	// socket per path targeting that path's concentrator address, plus the WG peer.
119	func edgeConfigBody(psk, edgePriv, concPub string, paths []pathSpec) string {
120		var b strings.Builder
121		for _, p := range paths {
122			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\ndest_addr = \"%s:%d\"\n\n", p.name, p.edgeIP, p.concIP, listenPort)
123		}
124		return fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
125	psk = "%s"
126	
127	%s[wireguard]
128	private_key = "%s"
129	
130	[[wireguard.peers]]
131	public_key = "%s"
132	endpoint = "%s:%d"
133	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
134	
135	[log]
136	level = "error"
137	`, psk, b.String(), edgePriv, concPub, paths[0].concIP, listenPort, concInner)
138	}
139	
140	// concConfigBody renders the concentrator TOML for the given path set.
141	func concConfigBody(psk, concPriv, edgePub string, paths []pathSpec) string {
142		var b strings.Builder
143		for _, p := range paths {
144			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\n\n", p.name, p.concIP)
145		}
146		return fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
147	psk = "%s"
148	
149	%s[wireguard]
150	private_key = "%s"
151	listen_port = %d
152	
153	[[wireguard.peers]]
154	public_key = "%s"
155	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
156	
157	[log]
158	level = "error"
159	`, psk, b.String(), concPriv, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner)
160	}
161	
162	// writeReload rewrites an existing config file in place (mode 0600) so the daemon's
163	// SIGHUP reload re-reads the new path set from the same path.
164	func writeReload(t *testing.T, path, body string) {
165		t.Helper()
166		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(body), 0o600); err != nil {
167			t.Fatalf("rewrite config %s: %v", path, err)
168		}
169	}
170	
171	// sighup delivers SIGHUP to a running daemon to trigger its config reload.
172	func sighup(t *testing.T, p *proc) {
173		t.Helper()
174		if p.cmd.Process == nil {
175			t.Fatalf("%s has no process to signal", p.name)
176		}
177		if err := p.cmd.Process.Signal(syscall.SIGHUP); err != nil {
178			t.Fatalf("SIGHUP %s: %v", p.name, err)
179		}
180		time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond) // let the reload apply
181	}

1	//go:build e2e

3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"os/exec"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11		"testing"
12		"time"
13	)
14	
15	// pathSpec describes one emulated WAN uplink between the edge and concentrator
16	// namespaces: a veth pair carrying a /24, with netem delay+jitter on the edge
17	// egress. The bandwidth cap (rateMbit) and controlled loss (lossPct) are
18	// OPTIONAL config-time impairments: both default to zero, so the DefaultPaths
19	// topology stays uncapped and lossless and every existing P0/P1 e2e test runs
20	// unchanged. A non-zero rateMbit makes the LINK — not the single-core userspace
21	// WG crypto — the bottleneck ONLY when rateMbit sits below the EXECUTING host's
22	// measured in-fixture tunnel ceiling. That ceiling is CPU/PPS-bound (both daemons
23	// plus the load generator share the host's cores), a lower bound that scales with
24	// core count, NOT a link-throughput spec: ~12–46 Mbit/s single-flow on a 1-vCPU
25	// aarch64 host (docs/p0-findings.md:216-225), ~13 Mbit/s single-path (up to
26	// ~47–87 Mbit/s FEC single-flow) on a 4-vCPU amd64 host. Sizing rule: cap < ceiling
27	// for single-path, 2×cap < ceiling for aggregation. Below the ceiling
28	// a standing queue can form for bufferbloat/pacing (T21/T23) work; a non-zero
29	// lossPct injects uniform egress loss at Setup time for FEC-recovery (T25/T29)
30	// work.
31	type pathSpec struct {
32		name     string
33		edgeIP   string
34		concIP   string
35		edgeVeth string // <=15 chars
36		concVeth string
37		delayMs  int
38		jitterMs int
39		rateMbit int     // optional per-path bandwidth cap (netem rate); 0 = uncapped
40		lossPct  float64 // optional config-time uniform egress loss (netem loss); 0 = lossless
41	
42		// deferEdgeAddr, when true, makes Setup create and bring up the edge veth WITHOUT
43		// assigning edgeIP to it: the interface exists (so tc/netem still applies and the
44		// link is up) but the configured source_addr is not yet owned by any interface —
45		// the well-formed-but-not-yet-assignable condition T51's tolerant Open() defers and
46		// T55's background reconcile later promotes (T60). AddEdgeAddr adds the withheld
47		// address later. Zero value (false) is the default for every existing path, so
48		// Setup's behaviour for DefaultPaths and every other caller is unchanged.
49		deferEdgeAddr bool
50	}
51	
52	// DefaultPaths are the two emulated links: Starlink-like (low latency, jittery)
53	// and 5G-like (higher latency, stable).
54	var DefaultPaths = []pathSpec{
55		{name: "starlink", edgeIP: "10.100.1.1", concIP: "10.100.1.2", edgeVeth: "wbAe", concVeth: "wbAc", delayMs: 45, jitterMs: 10},
56		{name: "cellular", edgeIP: "10.100.2.1", concIP: "10.100.2.2", edgeVeth: "wbBe", concVeth: "wbBc", delayMs: 64, jitterMs: 0},
57	}
58	
59	// Topology is a two-namespace netns/netem fixture. The edge side is the current
60	// (re-exec'd) network namespace; the concentrator side is a child process's
61	// network namespace, addressed by PID so no writable /run/netns is required.
62	type Topology struct {
63		t      *testing.T
64		holder *exec.Cmd
65		pid    int
66		paths  []pathSpec
67	}
68	
69	// Setup builds the two-path topology from DefaultPaths (uncapped, lossless). It
70	// requires CAP_NET_ADMIN, which the e2e TestMain provides via an unprivileged
71	// user+net namespace (`unshare -Urmn`).
72	func Setup(t *testing.T) *Topology {
73		return SetupWithPaths(t, DefaultPaths)
74	}
75	
76	// SetupWithPaths builds the topology from an explicit path set, allowing a test
77	// to opt into the optional per-path bandwidth cap (rateMbit) and/or config-time
78	// loss (lossPct). DefaultPaths leaves both zero, so Setup is unchanged.
79	func SetupWithPaths(t *testing.T, paths []pathSpec) *Topology {
80		t.Helper()
81		top := &Topology{t: t, paths: paths}
82	
83		// Hold the concentrator network namespace open with a sleeping child.
84		top.holder = exec.Command("unshare", "-n", "sleep", "600")
85		if err := top.holder.Start(); err != nil {
86			t.Fatalf("start concentrator netns holder: %v", err)
87		}
88		top.pid = top.holder.Process.Pid
89		top.waitForNetns()
90	
91		pid := strconv.Itoa(top.pid)
92		top.run("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up")
93		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up")
94		// Pin net.ipv4.ip_nonlocal_bind=0 in the edge (test-process) network namespace, so
95		// a path whose source_addr is NOT assigned to any interface deterministically fails
96		// to bind (EADDRNOTAVAIL) instead of succeeding. That non-local-bind FAILURE is the
97		// premise the tolerant-startup tests (T60) rest on: a not-yet-assignable source_addr
98		// must DEFER (T51) and a config where NO path can bind must fail Open. A host that
99		// leaves the sysctl at 1 (or runs permissively) lets a non-local bind SUCCEED, which
100		// silently voids both premises (observed nondeterministic on a real host: a deferred
101		// path binding instead of deferring, or a zero-bindable daemon coming up). A fresh
102		// netns SHOULD default to 0, but pin it explicitly rather than trust the default.
103		// This affects ONLY non-local binds, so every other e2e test — which binds real,
104		// assigned addresses — is byte-for-byte unaffected. The concentrator netns needs no
105		// pin: every concentrator source_addr (concIP) IS assigned there (deferEdgeAddr only
106		// withholds the EDGE address), so the concentrator never binds a non-local address.
107		disableNonlocalBind(top.t)
108		for _, p := range top.paths {
109			// Idempotent pre-delete: the veth names are FIXED per path, so a prior subtest's
110			// teardown racing the kernel's async netns/veth reap can leave the edge veth behind,
111			// making `ip link add` fail with EEXIST ("File exists"). Deleting the pair first
112			// (ignore-if-absent) makes Setup robust to reused fixed names across sequential
113			// subtests. Deleting the edge end removes its peer too.
114			_ = top.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", p.edgeVeth)
115			top.run("ip", "link", "add", p.edgeVeth, "type", "veth", "peer", "name", p.concVeth)
116			top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.concVeth, "netns", pid)
117			if !p.deferEdgeAddr {
118				top.run("ip", "addr", "add", p.edgeIP+"/24", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
119			}
120			top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
121			top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", p.concIP+"/24", "dev", p.concVeth)
122			top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", p.concVeth, "up")
123			qargs := append([]string{"qdisc", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "root", "netem"}, top.netemArgs(p)...)
124			top.run("tc", qargs...)
125		}
126		t.Cleanup(top.Teardown)
127		return top
128	}
129	
130	// waitForNetns blocks until the holder's network namespace is observable.
131	func (top *Topology) waitForNetns() {
132		path := fmt.Sprintf("/proc/%d/ns/net", top.pid)
133		for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
134			if _, err := os.Stat(path); err == nil {
135				return
136			}
137			time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
138		}
139		top.t.Fatalf("concentrator netns %s never appeared", path)
140	}
141	
142	// netemArgs builds the netem parameter list for a path's baseline impairment
143	// profile: delay/jitter, then its configured loss (lossPct) and bandwidth cap
144	// (rateMbit) if set. For a DefaultPaths entry (both zero) this reduces to the
145	// prior delay/jitter-only output, so existing qdisc setup is byte-identical.
146	func (top *Topology) netemArgs(p pathSpec) []string {
147		return top.netemArgsWithLoss(p, p.lossPct)
148	}
149	
150	// netemArgsWithLoss is netemArgs with the loss percentage overridden — used by
151	// InjectLoss to set runtime loss while preserving delay/jitter and any rate cap.
152	// netem accepts the options in delay/loss/rate order.
153	func (top *Topology) netemArgsWithLoss(p pathSpec, lossPct float64) []string {
154		args := []string{"delay", fmt.Sprintf("%dms", p.delayMs)}
155		if p.jitterMs > 0 {
156			args = append(args, fmt.Sprintf("%dms", p.jitterMs))
157		}
158		if lossPct > 0 {
159			args = append(args, "loss", fmt.Sprintf("%g%%", lossPct))
160		}
161		if p.rateMbit > 0 {
162			args = append(args, "rate", fmt.Sprintf("%dmbit", p.rateMbit))
163		}
164		return args
165	}
166	
167	// path looks up a path by name, failing the test if absent.
168	func (top *Topology) path(name string) pathSpec {
169		for _, p := range top.paths {
170			if p.name == name {
171				return p
172			}
173		}
174		top.t.Fatalf("unknown path %q", name)
175		return pathSpec{}
176	}
177	
178	// RTT pings the concentrator end of the named path from the edge and returns the
179	// average round-trip time in milliseconds.
180	func (top *Topology) RTT(name string, count int) float64 {
181		p := top.path(name)
182		out := top.runOut("ping", "-c", strconv.Itoa(count), "-i", "0.2", "-W", "1", p.concIP)
183		// rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 37.264/48.520/82.043/19.354 ms
184		idx := strings.Index(out, "min/avg/max")
185		if idx < 0 {
186			top.t.Fatalf("path %q: no rtt line in ping output:\n%s", name, out)
187		}
188		eq := strings.Index(out[idx:], "=")
189		fields := strings.Fields(out[idx+eq+1:])
190		nums := strings.Split(fields[0], "/")
191		avg, err := strconv.ParseFloat(nums[1], 64)
192		if err != nil {
193			top.t.Fatalf("path %q: parse avg rtt %q: %v", name, nums[1], err)
194		}
195		return avg
196	}
197	
198	// Reachable reports whether the concentrator end of the named path answers pings.
199	func (top *Topology) Reachable(name string, count int) bool {
200		p := top.path(name)
201		return top.tryRun("ping", "-c", strconv.Itoa(count), "-i", "0.2", "-W", "1", p.concIP) == nil
202	}
203	
204	// InjectLoss sets uniform egress loss (percent) on the named path at runtime,
205	// preserving its delay/jitter profile and any configured bandwidth cap.
206	func (top *Topology) InjectLoss(name string, pct float64) {
207		p := top.path(name)
208		args := append([]string{"qdisc", "change", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "root", "netem"}, top.netemArgsWithLoss(p, pct)...)
209		top.run("tc", args...)
210	}
211	
212	// ClearLoss restores the named path to its configured baseline impairment
213	// (delay/jitter, plus any config-time loss and bandwidth cap). For a DefaultPaths
214	// entry that is delay/jitter only.
215	func (top *Topology) ClearLoss(name string) {
216		p := top.path(name)
217		args := append([]string{"qdisc", "change", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "root", "netem"}, top.netemArgs(p)...)
218		top.run("tc", args...)
219	}
220	
221	// Blackhole brings the named path's edge link down, simulating a WAN death (used
222	// by the P1 failover test).
223	func (top *Topology) Blackhole(name string) {
224		p := top.path(name)
225		top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "down")
226	}
227	
228	// Restore brings the named path's edge link back up.
229	func (top *Topology) Restore(name string) {
230		p := top.path(name)
231		top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
232	}
233	
234	// Readdress replaces the edge-side address of the named path, simulating an edge
235	// public-IP change (used by the P1 roaming test). newEdgeIP must be a bare IPv4
236	// address in the path's /24.
237	func (top *Topology) Readdress(name, newEdgeIP string) {
238		p := top.path(name)
239		top.run("ip", "addr", "flush", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
240		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", newEdgeIP+"/24", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
241		top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
242	}
243	
244	// AddEdgeAddr adds the named path's configured edge-side address to its (already up)
245	// edge veth, WITHOUT flushing any existing address first (T60). It is the companion
246	// to a path built with deferEdgeAddr: that path's interface starts addressless, so
247	// this simulates the address becoming assignable after boot (e.g. a 5G modem's DHCP
248	// lease completing) for the T55 background reconciler to observe. Unlike Readdress —
249	// which flushes THEN re-adds, for the T16 re-roam case where an address is replaced —
250	// this only ADDS, because here there is nothing to flush.
251	func (top *Topology) AddEdgeAddr(name string) {
252		p := top.path(name)
253		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", p.edgeIP+"/24", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
254	}
255	
256	// nonlocalBindProc is the per-netns sysctl controlling whether a socket may bind a
257	// source address that is NOT assigned to any local interface. 0 (the value the
258	// tolerant-startup tests pin) makes such a bind fail EADDRNOTAVAIL; 1 lets it
259	// succeed. It is a plain proc file, so it is written/read directly rather than via
260	// the `sysctl` binary (one fewer external dependency).
261	const nonlocalBindProc = "/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_nonlocal_bind"
262	
263	// disableNonlocalBind pins ip_nonlocal_bind=0 in the CURRENT (edge/test) network
264	// namespace and asserts the value took, failing the test loudly if it cannot be set.
265	// A hard failure here — rather than proceeding — is deliberate: the tolerant-startup
266	// premise (a non-local bind must fail) is silently voided if the sysctl stays at 1,
267	// so an un-pinnable environment must surface as a test failure, never a false pass.
268	// os.WriteFile targets THIS process's netns, which is the edge/test namespace (the
269	// TestMain re-exec unshared it), exactly where the not-yet-assignable source_addr
270	// binds live. Writing a per-netns net sysctl needs the same privilege the fixture's
271	// veth/netem setup already requires, so anywhere the netns tier runs, this succeeds.
272	func disableNonlocalBind(t *testing.T) {
273		t.Helper()
274		if err := os.WriteFile(nonlocalBindProc, []byte("0\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
275			t.Fatalf("pin %s=0: %v (the tolerant-startup tests need a non-local bind to fail EADDRNOTAVAIL; without this a permissive host silently voids the premise)", nonlocalBindProc, err)
276		}
277		got, err := os.ReadFile(nonlocalBindProc)
278		if err != nil {
279			t.Fatalf("read back %s: %v", nonlocalBindProc, err)
280		}
281		if v := strings.TrimSpace(string(got)); v != "0" {
282			t.Fatalf("%s = %q after pin, want 0 — a non-local bind would still succeed and void the zero-bindable/deferred premise", nonlocalBindProc, v)
283		}
284	}
285	
286	// bringLoopbackUp brings the loopback interface UP in the CURRENT (edge/test)
287	// network namespace. It is a second, INDEPENDENT precondition for a non-local bind
288	// to fail EADDRNOTAVAIL: on this kernel the rejection fires only when at least one
289	// interface is UP — empirically, with lo DOWN a bind to a non-local address SUCCEEDS
290	// even at ip_nonlocal_bind=0 (kernel probe: lo-up+pin0 -> EADDRNOTAVAIL; lo-down+pin0
291	// -> BIND_OK; lo-up+pin1 -> BIND_OK). So the zero-bindable premise needs BOTH lo UP
292	// and the sysctl pinned to 0. SetupWithPaths already brings lo up for topology tests
293	// (line above), which is why the zero-bindable subtest passed only when it happened to
294	// run AFTER one; building no topology itself, it must bring lo up explicitly to hold
295	// regardless of subtest execution order. Idempotent (setting an already-up lo up is a
296	// no-op). Runs in this process's netns (the TestMain re-exec unshared it), like run().
297	func bringLoopbackUp(t *testing.T) {
298		t.Helper()
299		if out, err := exec.Command("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up").CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
300			t.Fatalf("bring lo up in the test netns: %v\n%s", err, out)
301		}
302	}
303	
304	// QdiscShow returns `tc qdisc show` for the named path (for assertions/debug).
305	func (top *Topology) QdiscShow(name string) string {
306		p := top.path(name)
307		return top.runOut("tc", "qdisc", "show", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
308	}
309	
310	// LinkExists reports whether the edge-side veth of the named path still exists.
311	func (top *Topology) LinkExists(name string) bool {
312		p := top.path(name)
313		return top.tryRun("ip", "link", "show", p.edgeVeth) == nil
314	}
315	
316	// Teardown removes the topology. Killing the holder drops the concentrator netns
317	// (and its veth ends); the edge-side veths are deleted explicitly. Idempotent.
318	func (top *Topology) Teardown() {
319		for _, p := range top.paths {
320			_ = top.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", p.edgeVeth)
321		}
322		if top.holder != nil && top.holder.Process != nil {
323			_ = top.holder.Process.Kill()
324			_, _ = top.holder.Process.Wait()
325			top.holder = nil
326		}
327	}
328	
329	func (top *Topology) run(name string, args ...string) {
330		top.t.Helper()
331		if out, err := exec.Command(name, args...).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
332			top.t.Fatalf("%s %s: %v\n%s", name, strings.Join(args, " "), err, out)
333		}
334	}
335	
336	func (top *Topology) tryRun(name string, args ...string) error {
337		return exec.Command(name, args...).Run()
338	}
339	
340	func (top *Topology) runOut(name string, args ...string) string {
341		top.t.Helper()
342		out, err := exec.Command(name, args...).CombinedOutput()
343		if err != nil {
344			top.t.Fatalf("%s %s: %v\n%s", name, strings.Join(args, " "), err, out)
345		}
346		return string(out)
347	}
348	
349	// iperfListenTimeout bounds waitIperfListen: how long to wait for the one-shot
350	// iperf3 server to reach the LISTEN state before failing the test.
351	const iperfListenTimeout = 5 * time.Second
352	
353	// waitIperfListen polls, inside the concentrator network namespace, for a TCP
354	// socket in the LISTEN state on the given port, and returns once it appears (or
355	// fails the test at iperfListenTimeout). It reads kernel socket state via
356	// `ss -ltn` and NEVER connects: the iperf3 servers here run one-shot (`-s -1`),
357	// so a probe-connect would consume the server's single accept and break the real
358	// client. It replaces the prior fixed sleeps at the iperf3Mbps and rttUnderLoad
359	// call sites, which raced a slow bind under load into 'connection refused' (D3).
360	func (top *Topology) waitIperfListen(t *testing.T, port int) {
361		t.Helper()
362		suffix := fmt.Sprintf(":%d", port)
363		deadline := time.Now().Add(iperfListenTimeout)
364		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
365			// ss -ltn columns: State Recv-Q Send-Q Local-Address:Port Peer-Address:Port.
366			out, err := exec.Command("nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "ss", "-ltn").CombinedOutput()
367			if err == nil {
368				for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
369					fields := strings.Fields(line)
370					if len(fields) >= 4 && strings.HasSuffix(fields[3], suffix) {
371						return
372					}
373				}
374			}
375			time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)
376		}
377		t.Fatalf("iperf3 server never reached LISTEN on port %d within %s", port, iperfListenTimeout)
378	}
379	
380	// nsenter runs a command inside the concentrator network namespace.
381	func (top *Topology) nsenter(args ...string) {
382		top.t.Helper()
383		full := append([]string{"-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n"}, args...)
384		top.run("nsenter", full...)
385	}

1	//go:build e2e

3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"os/exec"
10		"testing"
11	)
12	
13	// nsEnvMarker guards the one-shot re-exec below.
14	const nsEnvMarker = "WANBOND_E2E_NS"
15	
16	// TestMain re-execs the e2e test binary inside a FRESH network namespace so the
17	// fixtures never touch the host's networking. Under real root (the sudo target)
18	// it uses a plain mount+net namespace; unprivileged (the sandbox) it uses an
19	// unprivileged user+net namespace, which still grants CAP_NET_ADMIN for veth and
20	// netem. The PID-addressed peer namespace in netns.go needs no writable
21	// /run/netns, so this works in both environments.
22	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
23		if os.Getenv(nsEnvMarker) == "" {
24			self, err := os.Executable()
25			if err != nil {
26				fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "e2e: cannot find test binary:", err)
27				os.Exit(1)
28			}
29			unshareArgs := []string{"-Urmn"}
30			if os.Geteuid() == 0 {
31				unshareArgs = []string{"-mn"}
32			}
33			args := append([]string{}, unshareArgs...)
34			args = append(args, "--", self)
35			args = append(args, os.Args[1:]...)
36	
37			cmd := exec.Command("unshare", args...)
38			cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), nsEnvMarker+"=1")
39			cmd.Stdin, cmd.Stdout, cmd.Stderr = os.Stdin, os.Stdout, os.Stderr
40			err = cmd.Run()
41			if err == nil {
42				os.Exit(0)
43			}
44			var ee *exec.ExitError
45			if errors.As(err, &ee) {
46				os.Exit(ee.ExitCode())
47			}
48			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "e2e: namespace re-exec failed:", err)
49			os.Exit(1)
50		}
51		os.Exit(m.Run())
52	}

386	### 3z. Full configuration reference (all keys)
387	
388	This is the exhaustive key list — every configuration key wanbond reads, in one
389	place. The example below is **edge-oriented**; concentrator-only keys are shown
390	and marked `CONCENTRATOR-ONLY`. Required keys are shown live; optional/defaulted
391	keys are shown **commented-out with their default value**. Uncommenting a key
392	and leaving it at the shown value is a no-op. The per-section notes after the
393	example capture the cross-field constraints a single example cannot express
394	inline. All of these are enforced at config load (`config.Load`), which fails
395	fast on the first violation — except the loopback and `allowed_ips` checks,
396	noted below.
397	
398	```toml
399	# ── top level ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
400	role = "edge"                      # REQUIRED. "edge" | "concentrator". Never
401	                                   #   inferred from other keys.
402	psk  = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # REQUIRED. 32 raw bytes, base64. Same value
403	                                   #   on both ends; keys the PSK-HMAC that
404	                                   #   authenticates outer PROBE/CONTROL frames.
405	
406	# ── paths: one [[paths]] block per WAN uplink; at least one is REQUIRED ───────
407	[[paths]]
408	name        = "starlink"           # REQUIRED. Stable, unique identifier.
409	source_addr = "192.168.1.10"       # REQUIRED. Bare local source IP the path's
410	                                   #   UDP socket binds to. Must be unique across
411	                                   #   paths (a shared source collides EADDRINUSE
412	                                   #   at the second bind). No default.
413	# dest_addr = "203.0.113.7:51820"  # OPTIONAL, edge-only meaning. Per-path
414	                                   #   concentrator endpoint (ip:port). Omit when
415	                                   #   one public IP fronts all uplinks (the
416	                                   #   peer's endpoint is reused). No default.
417	                                   #   Inert on the concentrator (it learns edge
418	                                   #   endpoints from traffic).
419	# link_bandwidth = "50Mbit"        # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared bottleneck
420	                                   #   bandwidth. SI bit/s: k/M/G = 1e3/1e6/1e9
421	                                   #   ("bit" may be written "bps"). Must be > 0.
422	                                   #   Used ONLY under weighted policy + pacing;
423	                                   #   inert otherwise. No default (undeclared).
424	# link_rtt = "45ms"                # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared baseline RTT
425	                                   #   (Go duration). REQUIRED (> 0) when
426	                                   #   link_bandwidth is set under weighted
427	                                   #   pacing; ignored otherwise. No default.
428	
429	# A second uplink (edge). Repeat the block per path.
430	[[paths]]
431	name        = "5g"
432	source_addr = "192.168.2.10"
433	
434	# ── wireguard: inner tunnel key material ─────────────────────────────────────
435	[wireguard]
436	private_key = "<base64 32-byte private key>"  # REQUIRED (both roles).
437	# listen_port = 51820              # CONCENTRATOR-ONLY: REQUIRED (> 0) there;
438	                                   #   omit / leave 0 on the edge. uint16
439	                                   #   (< 1024 needs CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE).
440	
441	# ── wireguard peers: at least one [[wireguard.peers]] is REQUIRED ─────────────
442	[[wireguard.peers]]
443	public_key = "<base64 peer public key>"  # REQUIRED. base64 32-byte key.
444	endpoint = "203.0.113.7:51820"     # EDGE: REQUIRED (this OR `endpoints`).
445	                                   #   ip:port literal only, no DNS.

### 3z. Full configuration reference (all keys)

This is the exhaustive key list — every configuration key wanbond reads, in one
place. The example below is **edge-oriented**; concentrator-only keys are shown
and marked `CONCENTRATOR-ONLY`. Required keys are shown live; optional/defaulted
keys are shown **commented-out with their default value**. Uncommenting a key
and leaving it at the shown value is a no-op. The per-section notes after the
example capture the cross-field constraints a single example cannot express
inline. All of these are enforced at config load (`config.Load`), which fails
fast on the first violation — except the loopback and `allowed_ips` checks,
noted below.

```toml
# ── top level ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role = "edge"                      # REQUIRED. "edge" | "concentrator". Never
                                   #   inferred from other keys.
psk  = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # REQUIRED. 32 raw bytes, base64. Same value
                                   #   on both ends; keys the PSK-HMAC that
                                   #   authenticates outer PROBE/CONTROL frames.

# ── paths: one [[paths]] block per WAN uplink; at least one is REQUIRED ───────
[[paths]]
name        = "starlink"           # REQUIRED. Stable, unique identifier.
source_addr = "192.168.1.10"       # REQUIRED. Bare local source IP the path's
                                   #   UDP socket binds to. Must be unique across
                                   #   paths (a shared source collides EADDRINUSE
                                   #   at the second bind). No default.
# dest_addr = "203.0.113.7:51820"  # OPTIONAL, edge-only meaning. Per-path
                                   #   concentrator endpoint (ip:port). Omit when
                                   #   one public IP fronts all uplinks (the
                                   #   peer's endpoint is reused). No default.
                                   #   Inert on the concentrator (it learns edge
                                   #   endpoints from traffic).
# link_bandwidth = "50Mbit"        # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared bottleneck
                                   #   bandwidth. SI bit/s: k/M/G = 1e3/1e6/1e9
                                   #   ("bit" may be written "bps"). Must be > 0.
                                   #   Used ONLY under weighted policy + pacing;
                                   #   inert otherwise. No default (undeclared).
# link_rtt = "45ms"                # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared baseline RTT
                                   #   (Go duration). REQUIRED (> 0) when
                                   #   link_bandwidth is set under weighted
                                   #   pacing; ignored otherwise. No default.

# A second uplink (edge). Repeat the block per path.
[[paths]]
name        = "5g"
source_addr = "192.168.2.10"

# ── wireguard: inner tunnel key material ─────────────────────────────────────
[wireguard]
private_key = "<base64 32-byte private key>"  # REQUIRED (both roles).
# listen_port = 51820              # CONCENTRATOR-ONLY: REQUIRED (> 0) there;
                                   #   omit / leave 0 on the edge. uint16
                                   #   (< 1024 needs CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE).

# ── wireguard peers: at least one [[wireguard.peers]] is REQUIRED ─────────────
[[wireguard.peers]]
public_key = "<base64 peer public key>"  # REQUIRED. base64 32-byte key.
endpoint = "203.0.113.7:51820"     # EDGE: REQUIRED (this OR `endpoints`).

1	// Command wanbond is the wanbond tunnel daemon. A single binary serves both
2	// roles (edge and concentrator); the role is selected from configuration at
3	// runtime, not by which binary is invoked.
4	package main
5	
6	import (
7		"flag"
8		"fmt"
9		"os"
10		"os/signal"
11		"syscall"
12	
13		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/config"
14		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/device"
15		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/log"
16	)
17	
18	// version is overridden at build time via -ldflags "-X main.version=...".
19	var version = "dev"
20	
21	func main() {
22		if err := run(os.Args[1:]); err != nil {
23			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "wanbond:", err)
24			os.Exit(1)
25		}
26	}
27	
28	// run is the testable entry point: it parses the command line, loads the
29	// configuration, brings the tunnel up for the configured role, and blocks until
30	// a termination signal, then tears the tunnel down.
31	func run(args []string) error {
32		if len(args) == 1 && (args[0] == "version" || args[0] == "--version") {
33			fmt.Println("wanbond", version)
34			return nil
35		}
36	
37		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("wanbond", flag.ContinueOnError)
38		configPath := fs.String("config", "", "path to the TOML configuration file (mode 0600)")
39		if err := fs.Parse(args); err != nil {
40			return err
41		}
42		if *configPath == "" {
43			return fmt.Errorf("--config is required (or `wanbond version`)")
44		}
45	
46		cfg, err := config.Load(*configPath)
47		if err != nil {
48			return err
49		}
50	
51		lg, err := log.New(cfg.Log.Level, os.Stderr)
52		if err != nil {
53			return err
54		}
55		main := lg.Component("main")
56		main.Info("wanbond starting", "version", version, "role", string(cfg.Role))
57	
58		tun, err := device.Up(cfg, lg)
59		if err != nil {
60			return err
61		}
62		defer tun.Close()
63	
64		main.Info("tunnel interface up", "interface", tun.Name(), "role", string(cfg.Role))
65	
66		// SIGINT/SIGTERM terminate; SIGHUP reloads the config file to add/remove paths
67		// on the running tunnel (T30). SIGHUP is idiomatic for a daemon config reload and
68		// needs no extra socket or privilege.
69		sig := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
70		signal.Notify(sig, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM, syscall.SIGHUP)
71		stopped := deviceStopped(tun)
72		for {
73			select {
74			case s := <-sig:
75				if s == syscall.SIGHUP {
76					reloadTunnel(main, tun, *configPath)
77					continue
78				}
79				main.Info("shutting down", "signal", s.String())
80				return nil
81			case <-stopped:
82				// Unrecoverable engine teardown: fail loud so the exit status reflects it
83				// and a supervisor (systemd Restart=on-failure) restarts the daemon.
84				return fmt.Errorf("tunnel device stopped unexpectedly")
85			}
86		}
87	}
88	
89	// reloadTunnel re-reads and validates the config file and applies its path diff to
90	// the running tunnel. A bad reload (unreadable, insecure-mode, invalid, or a failed
91	// path change) is LOGGED and the running tunnel is left intact — a reload must never
92	// tear down a live session. config.Load performs the fail-fast validation.
93	func reloadTunnel(lg log.Logger, tun *device.Tunnel, configPath string) {
94		cfg, err := config.Load(configPath)
95		if err != nil {
96			lg.Error("config reload rejected; keeping running config", "error", err.Error())
97			return
98		}
99		if err := tun.Reload(cfg); err != nil {
100			lg.Error("config reload failed to apply; tunnel still running", "error", err.Error())
101			return
102		}
103		lg.Info("config reloaded")
104	}
105	
106	// deviceStopped returns a channel that closes when the tunnel device tears down
107	// on its own (an unrecoverable engine error), so run can exit rather than block
108	// on a dead device.
109	func deviceStopped(t *device.Tunnel) <-chan struct{} {
110		done := make(chan struct{})
111		go func() {
112			t.Wait()
113			close(done)
114		}()
115		return done
116	}

11	// testKey returns a syntactically valid base64 32-byte key for fixtures.
12	func testKey(b byte) string {
13		raw := make([]byte, keyLen)
14		for i := range raw {
15			raw[i] = b
16		}
17		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(raw)
18	}

20	// writeConfig writes body to a temp file with the given mode and returns its path.
21	func writeConfig(t *testing.T, mode os.FileMode, body string) string {
22		t.Helper()
